name: Wireless Intrusion Detection & Response
description: Detects and responds to wireless intrusion events including rogue access points, evil twin attacks, deauthentication floods, and unauthorized wireless clients. Riggs AI analyzes the wireless alert to identify the threat type and locate the rogue AP using signal triangulation data. Confirmed rogue APs are disabled via the wireless controller, the wired switch port is shut down, physical security is dispatched, and a security ticket is created. False positives from neighboring networks are documented and suppressed.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: Cisco Wireless LAN Controller
required_integrations.reason: Contain rogue AP and manage wireless security policies

required_integrations.name: Cisco Switch
required_integrations.reason: Shut down the wired switch port feeding the rogue access point

required_integrations.name: Jira
required_integrations.reason: Create security ticket for physical investigation and AP removal

required_integrations.name: Slack
required_integrations.reason: Alert SOC and physical security teams for rogue AP removal

config.description: Triggers when the WIDS/WIPS detects rogue access points, evil twin attacks, deauthentication floods, or unauthorized wireless activity

config.additional_instructions: Analyze the wireless alert: identify AP BSSID/SSID, channel, signal strength, vendor OUI, connected clients, and triangulated physical location. Determine if this is a rogue AP, evil twin, or neighboring network. Check if BSSID is in the authorized AP inventory.
config.description: AI-powered analysis of the wireless intrusion alert to classify the threat type, identify the rogue AP, and estimate physical location
Routes based on whether the wireless device is confirmed as a rogue AP or evil twin versus a neighboring network or authorized device
Activates wireless containment on the rogue AP through the WLC to deauthenticate connected clients and prevent new associations

parameters.comment: Port disabled — rogue AP BSSID: {{trigger_1.alert.rogue_bssid}} connected here

config.description: Disables the wired switch port feeding the rogue AP to cut its network connectivity entirely
Creates a security ticket for physical security to locate and remove the rogue wireless device
Alerts the SOC and physical security teams with rogue AP location for physical removal

parameters.note: Wireless alert for BSSID {{trigger_1.alert.rogue_bssid}} (SSID: {{trigger_1.alert.rogue_ssid}}) analyzed. Verdict: {{analyze_1.verdict}}. Determined to be a neighboring network or authorized device. Alert suppressed.

config.description: Suppresses false positive alerts from neighboring wireless networks or authorized devices
Wireless intrusion detection and response workflow complete
